rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainant Alec Berger'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Expenses (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT ALEC BERGER'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Expenses (Part 2 of 2). Varum argues in Opposition briefing that he has conducted a diligent search and produced the sought-after documents. But Varum's responses are not code compliant insofar as they fail either to state that a "diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand" and/or fail to explain why Varum was unable to comply and/or fail to "set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control" of the sought-after documents Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.230. Varum argues the tax documents are protected by the tax payer privilege. But Varum did not object to the production based on the tax payer privilege. He simply objected based on some generalized but unstated "privilege." Objections for privilege must specify the privilege. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.240 ("If an objection is based on a claim of privilege, the particular privilege invoked shall be stated."). On that basis the privilege was waived. Second, the privilege was waived when the objections were served late. "A party that fails to serve a timely response to the discovery request waives any objection to the request, including one based on privilege or the protection of attorney work product." Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pac. Healthcare Consultants, 148 Cal. App. 4th 390, 403-04 (2007); see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.300. Third, the privilege was waived the when Varum produced tax documents. Varum may not choose to produce tax documents favorable to his position while withholding tax documents that may be unfavorable to his position. Finally Varum has failed to produce any documents related to his real property. It is not credible that Varum would have recently transferred ownership of real property to his son but has somehow retained not a single document related to the property or its transfer. Varum fails to deny the fact of ownership of the real property or its transfer or otherwise offer an explanation for any of this in Opposition. Berger shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT ALEC BERGER's MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Alec Berger ("Berger") Motion to Compel Production of Documents is GRANTED. Insofar as Berger failed to provide notice, sanctions are DENIED. Cross-Defendant Gary Varum ("Varum") shall conduct a diligent search of documents in his or his agents' possession, custody or control, shall produce responsive documents, and shall serve verified, code compliant responses consistent with this Order. In response to a request for production of documents and things a responding party "shall state that the production . . . demanded will be allowed either in whole or in part." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.220; see also id. § 2031.240(a) ("If only part of an item or category of item in a demand . . . is objectionable, the response shall contain a statement of compliance, or a representation of inability to comply with respect to the remainder of that item or category."). In the alternative, a responding party may represent that it cannot "comply with the particular demand" and in that case "shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand" and shall further "specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer in the possession custody, or control of the responding party." Id. § 2031.230. Berger argues that Varum failed to provide a proper response as to document requests seeking financial information. In response, Varum fails to submit a Separate Statement opposing the motion, which may be an independent reason to strike opposition arguments. See Cal. R. Ct. Rule 3.1345; Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 893 (2008). Extending Varum every benefit of the doubt, the Court declines to strike the Opposition, and turns to Varum's briefing. (Continued to part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN V ANDREWS et al VS. TETRA TECH EC, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC D/B/A NEW WORLD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication. Denied. The federal enclave doctrine applies where the pertinent events giving rise to the plaintiff's claims occur on a federal enclave. The analytical key is where the work occurred, not where the employer made the decision to terminate. See Lockart v. MVM, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 1452, 1459. Here, pars. 15-21 of plaintiff's declaration create a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the pertinent events that gave rise to plaintiff's claims occurred on a federal enclave. Only part of Hunters Point is a federal enclave. Plaintiff states that based on defendant's map, the dangerous remediation work that he complained about and much of plaintiff's other work occurred outside of the enclave. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SUDI SCULL VS. DAMIAN PADRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. Defendant met its initial burden of establishing that the ambulance driver, Mr. Padro, was a licensed EMT responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 2-4.) Plaintiff does not present evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT or as to whether Mr. Padro was responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Therefore, pursuant to Canister v. Emergency Ambulance Service (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 388, the one-year statute of limitations provided for in the Medical Injury Compensation Reform Act (MICRA) applies. (CCP sec. 340.5.) Plaintiff's injuries occurred on July 20, 2012. (Complaint, par. MV-1.) The complaint was filed on February 6, 2014, beyond the statute of limitations. Alternatively, Defendant met its initial burden of establishing that the immunity provided for in Cal. Health and Safety Code sec. 1799.106 applies because Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Plaintiff is not alleging gross negligence. (UMF 22-23.) "Because the vicarious liability of the employer is wholly dependent or derived from the liability of the employee, any substantive defense that is available to the employee inures to the benefit of the employer." (Lathrop v. Healthcare Partners Medical Group (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1423.) Again, Plaintiff does not present evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT or as to whether Mr. Padro was responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Further, there is no evidence presented to raise a triable issue as to whether Mr. Padro acted with gross negligence. Lastly, Plaintiff's negligent entrustment claim fails as a matter of law, because there is no factual dispute that Mr. Padro was acting within the course and scope of his employment at the time of the accident, and therefore, Defendant is sued on a vicarious liability theory. If an employer admits vicarious liability for any negligent driving by its employee, the plaintiff cannot also pursue a negligent entrustment claim. (Diaz v. Carcamo (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1148, 1152.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: OSMAN KARAKUS VS. MG-252 CAL,LLC (DOING BUSINESS AS MICHAEL MINA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Mg-252 Cal, Llc And The Mina Group, Llc'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Mg-252 Cal, LLC And The Mina Group, LLC'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff. Granted. A natural person may be deposed only once. CCP 2025.610(a). Plaintiff was deposed on August 26, 2014 and the deposition was halted by defense counsel shortly after 5:00 p.m. that day. Absent an alternative stipulation between the parties, if defendant intended to further depose plaintiff, then the deposition was to have continued on the following business day, as per defendant's deposition notice. Sprague v. Equifax, Inc. (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 1012, 1041; Dunne on Depositions in California (Thompson Reuters Nov. 2014) §6:25 (relied upon by defendant). Defendant does not contend that any effort was made to do so, nor that plaintiff then refused to appear the following day. In the absence of an alternative stipulation between the parties, it was defendant's burden as the noticing party to timely seek completion of the deposition or to timely move to compel its completion. Defendant's four-month delay before seeking to complete plaintiff's deposition was substantial, and is not rendered reasonable by the fact the parties were discussing selection of a mediator; it is the responsibility of the parties to prepare for trial, and parties often discuss settlement or ADR in parallel with their preparation of the case. The delay was prejudicial to plaintiff in that defendants were able to analyze his initial transcript and undertake additional investigation based thereon, as well as obtain additional written discovery, before seeking to further depose plaintiff. Permitting such a considerable delay between sessions of a deponent without agreement of the parties effectively and impermissibly defeats the intent of CCP 2025.610(a). Sanctions are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defense counsel in the amount of $2,000.00 pursuant to CCP 2025.420(h). Defendants' opposition is without substantial justification, and the meet and confer as to the December 31 date unilaterally set months later for the further deposition was not in good faith, as defense counsel would not offer any explanation as to how much further time would be necessary or what topics he intended to pursue, although plaintiff offered to pursue a compromise in this respect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: OSMAN KARAKUS VS. MG-252 CAL,LLC (DOING BUSINESS AS MICHAEL MINA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 4, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. Granted. As to the inspection demands, Defendants or their counsel should immediately provide notice to Messers. Schantz and Davis of the inspection demands, RPD's 21-24 (if they have not already done so). Additionally, substantive, written responses in conformance with CCP must be submitted by Defendants as to the existence of any documents constituting or referring to any such performance reviews or disciplinary actions taken. The existence of such records is discoverable and the need of discovery by plaintiff to seek admissible evidence to prove his case on the merits is a compelling interest. As to the privilege log, the claim of privilege as to the 4/11/12 document is overruled. Sanctions are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defense counsel in the amount of $2,000.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
